---
title: "Carolina Yellow-eyed Grass (Xyris caroliniana)"
slug: "xyris-caroliniana"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/475304547/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Carolina Yellow-eyed Grass"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Liliopsida"
tax_order: "Poales"
tax_family: "Xyridaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Liliopsida", "Poales", "Xyridaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/475304547/medium.jpg" alt="Carolina Yellow-eyed Grass" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/damien6868">
damien6868</a> (CC BY) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/264683619">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Liliopsida
-   **Order:** Poales
-   **Family:** Xyridaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=170358)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common native perennial in Florida's moist upland sites, pine flatwoods, and bogs

## Natural history notes

A slender wetland plant characterized by its narrow, grass-like leaves and small yellow flower heads borne on upright stalks. In Florida, it is found in moist to wet habitats such as flatwoods, bogs, and savannas, often in acidic, sandy soils. It typically blooms during the warmer months and is most noticeable when in flower, attracting small pollinators.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/170358-Xyris-caroliniana)
-   [Local profile](https://www.flawildflowers.org/flower-friday-xyris-caroliniana/)
